cases.title: OrganizerAdmin publishes draft event within organization scope
preconditions: Draft event owned by organization org-1
OrganizerAdmin JWT with organizationId org-1
Event state is Draft and publish preconditions satisfied
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/publish with OrganizerAdmin Authorization bearer
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId} with same token
cases.expected: HTTP 200; event state transitions to Published; OrganizerAdmin role grants publish capability per permission matrix; authorization by role and org-owned event scope succeeds

cases.title: OrganizerStaff performs check-in on assigned event
preconditions: Event InProgress with active check-in window and Registered participant
OrganizerStaff JWT with assignedEventIds including eventId
Fresh Idempotency-Key header value
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/checkins with staff Authorization bearer and target registrationId
Verify check-in persisted for the registration
cases.expected: HTTP 200; check-in recorded with timestamp; staff operates within assigned event scope per permission matrix Check in participant Yes (assigned events)

cases.title: Participant registers and reads own registration status only
preconditions: Published event in RegistrationOpen with available capacity
Participant JWT with no active registration for the event
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with Participant Authorization bearer
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registration-status with same token
GET /api/v1/me/registrations with same token
cases.expected: Registration succeeds with Registered state; registration-status and /me/registrations reflect only the authenticated participant's records; Participant scope limited to own registration per scope constraints section 3

cases.title: OrganizerAdmin revokes eligibility with mandatory reason
preconditions: Completed event with evaluated eligibility for a registration
OrganizerAdmin JWT with scope over eventId
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/eligibility/{registrationId}/revoke with OrganizerAdmin bearer and body containing reasonCode and reasonText
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/eligibility with admin token
cases.expected: HTTP 200; eligibility state updated to Revoked; only OrganizerAdmin may override eligibility per permission matrix; sensitive action authorized by role with reason guardrail

cases.title: OrganizerStaff views paginated registrations on assigned event
preconditions: Event with multiple participant registrations
OrganizerStaff JWT with assignedEventIds including eventId
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20 with staff Authorization bearer
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with paginated envelope (items, page, pageSize, total, totalPages); operational registrations list returned for assigned event within authorized staff scope

cases.title: JWT role selection uses highest-privilege role when user has multiple roles
preconditions: User role rows include both Participant and OrganizerAdmin for same userId
selectJwtRole function available in auth validation module
steps: Call selectJwtRole with roles array containing Participant and OrganizerAdmin entries
Inspect returned selected role
cases.expected: Selected role is OrganizerAdmin; authorization decisions use highest-privilege role so organizer capabilities are not incorrectly downgraded to Participant

cases.title: Authorization pipeline validates role permission before event scope and domain execution
preconditions: Protected command handler wired with authorization decision pattern: authenticate, role, scope, state guard, execute
Real database test harness with transaction rollback
Authenticated Participant actor attempting organizer-only mutation
steps: Invoke organizer-only command (e.g. POST /events/{eventId}/publish) with valid Participant JWT and body
Confirm handler short-circuits at role permission step before scope validators
Query events table to confirm no state mutation occurred
cases.expected: HTTP 403 returned before event scope or state-transition validators run; no domain mutation or audit side effects; satisfies NFR-08 authorization by role at decision pattern step 2

cases.title: Unauthorized critical event publish blocked for non-admin roles
preconditions: Draft event owned by organization
Valid Participant or OrganizerStaff JWT (not OrganizerAdmin)
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/publish with non-admin Authorization bearer
Verify event state unchanged in database
cases.expected: HTTP 403 FORBIDDEN; event remains Draft; classified as P0 defect if publish succeeded per testing plan defect severity model unauthorized critical update

cases.title: Event scope check runs before domain mutation on staff commands
preconditions: Protected staff command handler wired with authorization decision pattern
Real database test harness with transaction rollback
OrganizerStaff JWT scoped to event B attempting mutation on unassigned event A
steps: Invoke staff check-in or registrations list handler with staff token targeting unassigned eventId
Confirm handler short-circuits at event scope step before state-transition validators
Verify no CheckinRecord or registration mutation rows created for unassigned event
cases.expected: HTTP 403 or 404 returned before domain transaction; no mutation or data exposure; satisfies NFR-08 authorization by event scope at decision pattern step 3

cases.title: 403 role denial responses use standard typed error envelope
preconditions: Participant JWT
Organizer-only endpoint available (e.g. POST /events/{eventId}/publish or GET /events/{eventId}/export)
steps: Invoke organizer-only endpoint with Participant Authorization bearer
Parse JSON error body
cases.expected: HTTP 403 with error.code FORBIDDEN, user-safe error.message, and requestId; no sensitive organizer data leaked; immediate fail feedback per NFR-13

cases.title: Participant cannot create or publish events
preconditions: Valid Participant JWT
No organizer role on actor account
steps: POST /api/v1/events with Participant Authorization bearer and valid draft payload
POST /api/v1/events/{existingEventId}/publish with Participant bearer on a draft event
cases.expected: Both requests return HTTP 403 FORBIDDEN; no event created or published; Create/edit event draft and Publish/pause event denied for Participant per permission matrix

cases.title: OrganizerStaff check-in on unassigned event is blocked
preconditions: Event A InProgress with Registered participant and active check-in window
OrganizerStaff JWT assigned only to event B (not event A)
Fresh Idempotency-Key header value
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventAId}/checkins with staff token and valid registrationId on event A
Verify event A registration state and check-in records unchanged
cases.expected: HTTP 403 or 404; no CheckinRecord created on event A; cross-event staff mutation blocked per OrganizerStaff assigned events scope constraint

cases.title: OrganizerStaff cannot revoke eligibility
preconditions: Completed event with evaluated eligibility for a registration
OrganizerStaff JWT assigned to eventId
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/eligibility/{registrationId}/revoke with staff Authorization bearer and valid reason payload
cases.expected: HTTP 403 FORBIDDEN; eligibility state unchanged; Override eligibility Revoked is OrganizerAdmin only per permission matrix

cases.title: Self-service signup provisions Participant role only
preconditions: No existing account for test email
Public signup endpoint available
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with valid email, password, displayName
Inspect JWT profile roles from response
Attempt POST /api/v1/events with returned JWT
cases.expected: Signup succeeds with Participant role only; POST /events returns HTTP 403; OrganizerAdmin and OrganizerStaff are not assignable via public signup per account provisioning section 3.1

cases.title: Participant cannot read organizer audit logs
preconditions: Event with persisted audit log entries
Valid Participant JWT with registration on the event
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/audit-logs with Participant Authorization bearer
cases.expected: HTTP 403 FORBIDDEN; audit log items and actor metadata not returned; Read audit logs denied for Participant per permission matrix

cases.title: Authenticated Participant blocked from organizer management routes
preconditions: Web app running with role-aware route guards
Browser authenticated as Participant via login flow
steps: Navigate directly to an organizer-only route (e.g. event create or organizer dashboard path)
Observe redirect or access-denied behavior
cases.expected: Organizer UI does not render for Participant; user is redirected or shown access denied; client-side role guard complements API RBAC per NFR-08

cases.title: Participant cannot access organizer export or dashboard endpoints
preconditions: Published event with operational data
Valid Participant JWT
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/dashboard with Participant Authorization bearer
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/export with Participant Authorization bearer
cases.expected: Both requests return HTTP 403 FORBIDDEN; no operational metrics or export payload exposed; Export reports and dashboard access denied for Participant per permission matrix

cases.title: assertEventScope rejects OrganizerStaff for unassigned event
preconditions: JwtPayload role OrganizerStaff with assignedEventIds not containing target eventId
Valid eventId UUID for another event
steps: Call assertEventScope(staffActor, unassignedEventId)
cases.expected: Throws FORBIDDEN or equivalent authorization error; OrganizerStaff may only operate on assigned events per scope constraints section 3

cases.title: Participant cannot register on behalf of another user
preconditions: Published event in RegistrationOpen with available capacity
Participant A JWT authenticated
Known Participant B userId distinct from actor
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with Participant A bearer and body participantId set to Participant B userId
Query registrations for Participant B
cases.expected: HTTP 403 FORBIDDEN or registration created only for authenticated actor (Participant A); Participant cannot affect another participant's registration records per scope constraint section 3

cases.title: OrganizerAdmin critical rule change after registration open requires authorized actor with reason
preconditions: Event in RegistrationOpen with active registrations
OrganizerAdmin JWT with scope over eventId
Capacity or rule config mutation endpoint available
steps: PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} or rule-config endpoint with OrganizerAdmin bearer changing capacity after registration opened
Include mandatory reasonCode and reasonText in request per sensitive action guardrails
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/audit-logs with OrganizerAdmin bearer
cases.expected: HTTP 200; config change applied; audit record includes actorId, actorRole OrganizerAdmin, before/after diff, and reason fields; only authorized admin within event scope may perform sensitive config change per roles-permissions sections 4 and 5
